📖 User Guide
Monthly Data Entry Enter actuals · budget auto-calculates from prior-year CPOR
Projection Planner Enter expected rooms sold → budget auto-projects using prior-year CPOR
Full Year
One Month
🗓 Budget Season Forecaster Aug–Dec forecast for current incomplete year
Planning in August? Jan–Jul actuals are known. The system estimates Aug–Dec using your YTD CPOR × projected rooms sold, giving you a full-year estimated actual — your true base for next year's CPOR.
How to use: Enter expected Rooms Sold per month for each future year. Budget columns auto-calculate from the prior year's CPOR. Leave cells blank to use the prior year's monthly rooms sold as default.
Multi-Year Overview 2024–2035 annual summary · Budget export
Budget Adjustments Price increases & special one-time items — applied automatically to budget calculations
Scoped increases: Each increase has an exact From and To date (year + month). Only months within that range are affected. Your actual data is never changed — only the budget for Laundry Supplies is adjusted.
⬆ Add Price Increase Period
⭐
Special / One-Time Items
Fixed flat amounts added to a budget line in specific months — completely independent from price increases
How it works: Define an item (e.g. Flat Iron Padding), pick which expense line, select the months it appears, set the fixed QAR amount and year range. The system adds this flat amount on top of the CPOR-based budget for those months only. A +Special tag appears on the card so you always know why the budget is higher.
＋ Add Special Item
Settings & Base Data Foundation of all budget calculations
📌 How this tab works: These are the foundation values that drive every budget calculation in the system. The 2024 actuals you enter here are divided by rooms sold to get the CPOR (Cost Per Occupied Room) — which then multiplies by each future year's rooms sold to auto-calculate budgets. Only change these if your base year actuals change.
📐 Budget Calculation Flow
Step 1
2024 Actuals
You enter these below
÷
Step 2
Rooms Sold 2024
You enter this below
=
Step 3
CPOR per Line
Auto-calculated below
×
Step 4
Future Rooms Sold
Entered in Monthly Entry
=
Result
Monthly Budget
Shows in all tabs
💡 Example: Laundry Supplies 2024 = QAR 324,939 ÷ 46,613 rooms = CPOR 6.9710 → If 2025 January has 4,650 rooms: Budget = 6.9710 × 4,650 = QAR 32,415
📊
Base Year Actuals — 2024
Enter the actual full-year expenses for 2024
ℹ️ These are your confirmed 2024 annual actuals. They form the base for all CPOR calculations. If 2024 actuals change, update here and all budgets recalculate automatically.
Chemical & laundry product costs
Maintenance & operational items
Paper, printing & office supplies
Full year 2024 occupied room nights
🧮
Derived CPOR → 2025 Budget Driver
Auto-calculated · Base ÷ Rooms Sold
ℹ️ These are read-only — calculated automatically from the values on the left. They represent how much each expense line costs per occupied room night in 2024. This CPOR becomes the budget driver for 2025.
🧴 Laundry Supplies CPOR 6.9710
Laundry Supplies ÷ Rooms Sold = QAR per room night
⚙️ Operating Supplies CPOR 1.0257
Operating Supplies ÷ Rooms Sold = QAR per room night
🖨️ Printing & Stationery CPOR 0.3988
Printing & Stationery ÷ Rooms Sold = QAR per room night
📊 Total CPOR 8.3955
Total of all 3 lines · Used as overall budget benchmark
🏨
Property Configuration — Doha
Four Seasons Hotel Doha · 237 rooms
ℹ️ The number of rooms determines the maximum possible room nights per month and year. This drives the occupancy % calculation everywhere in the system.
Four Seasons Hotel Doha · Total keys
Max Room Nights / Year 86,505
237 rooms × 365 days = 86,505 possible room nights per year
Occupancy formula: Rooms Sold ÷ (237 × Days in Month) × 100 = Occupancy %
Example: 4,650 rooms in January (31 days) = 4,650 ÷ 7,347 = 63.3%
🎨

Every colour has a meaning — used consistently across all tabs

Expense Line Manager
Rename lines · Set date ranges · Add new lines · Changes reflect everywhere
💡 How it works: You can rename any line freely — only the display name changes, your data is safe. Date range controls when a line appears — months outside the range show "—". Cannot delete a line that has data entered. Add new lines with a custom name, colour and date range — they appear in Monthly Entry, reports and exports automatically.

RS BudgetPro V3.0 — Complete Reference

User Guide & Formula Reference

All 12 tabs · All formulas with worked examples · New: Year Completion Forecast · Month Lock · Expense Line Manager · Four Seasons Hotel Doha Laundry 2024–2035

Four Seasons Hotel Doha 237 Rooms · 12 Tabs New: Year Forecast + Month Lock Expense Line Manager Prepared by Reda Salah
📑 Contents
1Core Formulas Explained
2Budget Cascade & Year Completion Forecast
3Month Lock System
4Tab-by-Tab Guide — All 12 Tabs
5Expense Line Manager
6Formula Quick Reference
7Colour System
8Frequently Asked Questions
1. Core Formulas Explained
CPOR — Cost Per Occupied Room
The most important metric
CPOR = Total Expenses ÷ Rooms Sold
2025: 416,082 ÷ 46,536 = 8.9411 — this becomes 2026 budget driver
Monthly Budget Calculation
How each month auto-calculates
Laundry = Prior CPOR × Rooms × Multiplier
Operating = (Prior CPOR × Rooms) + Special Items
Printing = Prior CPOR × Rooms
Jan 2026: 8.9411 × 4,137 = QAR 36,991 budget
Year Completion Forecast
August budgeting with incomplete base year
YTD CPOR = YTD Expenses ÷ YTD Rooms
Forecast = YTD CPOR × Remaining Projected Rooms
Est. Full Year = Actuals + Forecast
Aug 2026: 7 months actual QAR 298,000 (22,000 rooms) → YTD CPOR 13.55 → Aug-Dec proj 24,000 rooms → Forecast QAR 325,200 → Est. Full Year CPOR 9.14 used for 2027 budget
Price Multiplier
Supplier increases on Laundry Supplies
Multiplier = 1 + (Increase% ÷ 100)
Single +6%: 1.060 · Two stacked: 1.06×1.04 = 1.1024 (+10.24% total)
Occupancy %
Based only on months with data
Occ% = Rooms Sold ÷ (237 × Days) × 100
Jan: 4,650 ÷ 7,347 = 63.3% · Only months with rooms > 0 counted
Variance
Over or under budget
Variance = Actual − Budget
Variance% = (Actual − Budget) ÷ Budget × 100
+ = Over budget · − = Under (good)
58,342 − 46,771 = +11,571 (+24.7%)
KG Efficiency Metrics
Operational efficiency
Cost per KG = Expenses ÷ KG Processed
KG per Room Night = KG ÷ Rooms Sold
2025: 416,082 ÷ 1,925,291 = 0.22/KG · 1,925,291 ÷ 46,536 = 41.37 KG/RN
RPOR — Revenue Per Room
Revenue budget driver
RPOR = Total Revenue ÷ Rooms Sold
Projected = RPOR × (1+Growth%) × Rooms
283,116 ÷ 10,887 = RPOR 26.005 · At +5%: 26.005×1.05×4,500 = QAR 122,874
2. Budget Cascade & Year Completion Forecast
Each year's CPOR becomes the next year's budget driver. The system now uses Year Completion Forecast — if the base year is incomplete it blends actuals + forecasted remaining months for an accurate full-year estimate.
Budget Year CPOR Driver Formula Notes
2025Assumptions tab (2024 actuals)2024 Total ÷ 2024 RoomsManual base in Settings & Base Data
20262025 full-year actuals (all 12 months)2025 Total ÷ 2025 RoomsAuto when 2025 fully entered
20272026 estimated CPOR — actuals + forecast if partialgetYearEstimate(2026)Uses Year Completion Forecast if Aug–Dec missing
2028+Prior year estimated or actual CPORPrior Year Total ÷ Prior Year RoomsCascades automatically
August Budget Planning — Step by Step
1
Enter Jan–Jul actuals in Monthly Entry
2
Go to Projection Planner → enter expected rooms Aug–Dec in current year column
3
Check Year Completion Forecast panel at top — shows estimated full-year CPOR
4
The estimated CPOR (shown in purple) is used automatically for all next year budget calculations
5
Go to Multi-Year Overview → select next year → export 🖨 Budget PDF
6
Lock confirmed months: 🔒 Lock on each card or 🔒 Lock Year button
3. Month Lock System
After submitting to Finance, lock the month to prevent accidental edits. Locked months show in all reports — only inputs disabled.
🔒 Lock Individual Month
Each card in Monthly Entry has a 🔓 Lock button. Click → confirm → gold border + 🔒 + inputs disabled. Click again to unlock.
🔒 Lock Entire Year
🔒 Lock Year in Monthly Entry header — locks all months with data at once. 🔓 Unlock Year reverses all. Saves to Firebase immediately.
💡 Best practice: Lock each month after Finance confirms posting. Lock state saved to Firebase — persists across all devices.

User Guide
This guide

💡 Tip: Share with anyone who needs to understand the system.
5. Expense Line Manager
Found in Settings & Base Data tab → scroll to bottom → ⚡ Expense Line Manager.
Rename a Line
Edit name field → Save Line Config. Display name updates everywhere. Internal data keys never change.
Change Colour
Click colour picker → new colour → Save Line Config. Updates across all tabs and charts.
Add New Line
Click '➕ Add New Expense Line' → name, colour, budget type, CPOR inclusion, date range → Save.
Date Range
Active From/To = when line appears. Outside range = '—'. Existing data untouched. Max 8 lines total.
Delete a Line
Only custom lines with no data. Core 3 are 🔒 protected. '🔒 Has data' blocks deletion.
Budget Types
Auto-CPOR = calculated like existing. Manual = enter each month. Actuals Only = no budget comparison.
5a. Revenue Year Completion Forecast
Same problem as expenses — when budgeting 2027 revenue in August 2026, you only have 7 months of actual revenue. The Revenue Projection Tool now handles this with a Year Completion approach, but with an extra option: you already have a budget set for the remaining months.
📅 Select Last Actual Month
New dropdown in Step 1: "Base Year Actuals Available Up To"
Choose Up to July if starting in August
Choose Up to August if starting in September
System estimates remaining months automatically
📐 Two Scenarios Side by Side
Scenario A: YTD RPOR × projected remaining rooms
Best if actual RPOR differs from budget

Scenario B: Actuals + existing budget Aug–Dec
Best if you trust the set budget targets
August → 2027 Revenue Budget Workflow
1
Enter Jan–Jul 2026 revenue actuals in Revenue tab
2
Make sure Aug–Dec 2026 revenue budget is already set (from prior budget cycle)
3
Revenue tab → open Revenue Budget Projection Tool
4
Step 1: Budget Year = 2027, Base Year = 2026 → select "Up to July"
5
Revenue Year Completion panel appears — review Scenario A vs B for Laundry and Pearl side by side
6
Select your scenario → estimated RPOR shown and used for 2027 projection
7
Step 2: Growth % → Step 3: Generate → Step 4: Apply as Budget
💡 Key difference vs expenses: Revenue has two scenarios because you already have a set budget for Aug–Dec. Expenses only use YTD CPOR × rooms. Both scenarios show Laundry (FS Doha) and Pearl Valet (FS Pearl) side by side — select the one that better reflects your expectations.
6. Complete Formula Quick Reference
Metric Formula Worked Example Used In
CPOR ActualExpenses ÷ Rooms Sold416,082 ÷ 46,536 = 8.9411All tabs
CPOR Budget 20252024 Actuals ÷ 2024 Rooms324,939 ÷ 46,613 = 6.9710 (Laundry)Settings tab
CPOR Budget 2026+getYearEstimate(y-1) actuals+forecastEst. full year ÷ est. roomsAuto cascade
Year Completion ForecastActuals + (YTD CPOR × Remaining Rooms)298,000 + (13.55×24,000) = 623,200Projection Planner
Laundry BudgetPrior CPOR × Rooms × Multiplier8.9411 × 4,137 × 1.06 = 39,224Monthly Entry
Operating Budget(Prior CPOR × Rooms) + Special Items1.0257 × 4,137 + 0 = 4,243Monthly Entry
Printing BudgetPrior CPOR × Rooms0.3988 × 4,137 = 1,650Monthly Entry
Price Multiplier1 + (Increase% ÷ 100)1 + (6÷100) = 1.060Budget Adjustments
Stacked MultipliersMult1 × Mult21.06 × 1.04 = 1.1024Budget Adjustments
Occupancy%Rooms ÷ (237 × Days) × 1004,650 ÷ 7,347 × 100 = 63.3%All tabs
Cost per KGExpenses ÷ KG Processed416,082 ÷ 1,925,291 = 0.22Dashboard
KG per Room NightKG ÷ Rooms Sold1,925,291 ÷ 46,536 = 41.37Dashboard
VarianceActual − Budget58,342 − 46,771 = +11,571All tabs
Variance%(Actual − Budget) ÷ Budget × 10011,571 ÷ 46,771 = +24.7%Budget Report
RPORRevenue ÷ Rooms Sold283,116 ÷ 10,887 = 26.005Revenue tab
Projected RevenueRPOR × (1+Growth%) × Rooms26.005 × 1.05 × 4,500 = 122,874Revenue
YTD CPORYTD Expenses ÷ YTD Rooms200,000 ÷ 22,000 = 9.09Smart Insights
Full Year EstimateYTD + (YTD CPOR × Rem. Rooms)200,000 + (9.09×24,000) = 418,160Smart Insights

Violet

Total KG · Cost per KG · KG/RN

Coral

8. Frequently Asked Questions
1Why is 2027 budget wrong when 2026 is incomplete?▼
The Year Completion Forecast fixes this. Go to Projection Planner → enter projected rooms for remaining 2026 months → the panel calculates an estimated full-year CPOR → used automatically for 2027. No manual calculation needed.
2How do I lock a month after Finance submission?▼
In Monthly Entry, each card has a 🔓 Lock button. Click → confirm → gold border + 🔒 + inputs disabled. 🔒 Lock Year button locks all entered months at once. Lock saved to Firebase.
3How do I add a new expense line?▼
Settings & Base Data → scroll down → ⚡ Expense Line Manager → "➕ Add New Expense Line" → name, colour, date range, budget type → Save Line Config.
4How do I plan 2027 budget in August 2026?▼
1. Enter Jan–Jul actuals in Monthly Entry
2. Enter Aug–Dec projected rooms in Projection Planner
3. Check Year Completion Forecast panel — see estimated 2026 CPOR
4. Multi-Year Overview → export 2027 Budget PDF
5. Lock confirmed months
5My data disappeared in a different browser.▼
Data is in Firebase. Open from GitHub Pages URL and be online. Badge shows 🔥 LIVE when connected. Log in → data loads automatically.
6How do I clear projections for a specific month?▼
Projection Planner → One Month control: select year + month → 🗑 Clear. Both sync to Firebase and refresh instantly.
7What does "Copy Rooms → Monthly Entry" do?▼
Copies projected rooms from Projection Planner into Monthly Entry for future years — only fills empty months, never overwrites actuals.
8Can I rename Laundry Supplies?▼
Core line names are in Settings & Base Data top inputs. Custom lines can be renamed in Expense Line Manager. Internal data key (la, op, pr) never changes — only display name updates.
9How do I update with a new version from Claude?▼
Download HTML → GitHub repo → pencil icon → upload. Data in Firebase loads automatically after update.
10The card does not update while typing.▼
By design. Refreshes when you leave the field (Tab or click away). Prevents cursor jumping. Value saves instantly.
RS BudgetPro V3.0
Four Seasons Hotel Doha · Laundry Department · 2024–2035
Built and maintained by Reda Salah · Laundry Manager
